text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, small caps ripped, energy reversed, breadth decent. Indices rallied with IWM +2.52%, QQQ +1.66%, and SPY +1.02% as dip-buying in semis (SOXX +4.74%) and Fab 7 strength (+5.32%) overwhelmed XLE’s -2.43% flush. Positioning stayed defensive with SPY PCR 1.04 and QQQ PCR 1.35 while MOVE 85.3 and VIX 18.1 (vs 17.9 MA20) flagged lingering macro risk even as factor data kept VLUE in BULL regime (z=+1.70).

INDICES — VIX 18.1 ticked up vs 17.9 MA20. Returns ranked: SLV +2.74%, IWM +2.52%, IJR +2.03%, IJH +1.88%, QQQ +1.66%, GLD +1.43%, DIA +1.27%, RSP +1.10%, SPY +1.02%. STYLE: SMALL > LARGE by 0.86pp (small +2.52% vs large +1.66%). Most notable cross-asset move saw USO +2.46% alongside GLD -1.66%, while SPY and QQQ stayed above 200-day MAs, avoiding CTA sell triggers.

VOLUME — Index volume ran mixed with RSP 2.0x and DIA 1.5x elevated vs SPY 0.9x and QQQ 0.9x muted, signaling accumulation in equal-weight while cap-weight rallied on lower conviction. Sector volume showed XLB 2.0x, XLE 1.7x, and XLY 1.6x active versus XLRE 0.7x and XLU 0.8x quieter, consistent with rotation into cyclicals even as XLE sold off. Industry flows featured FCG 2.8x on a -2.38% drop (distribution) against BOAT 1.9x and XHB 1.9x on gains (accumulation).

SECTORS — XLY Discretionary +2.53%, XLK Technology +2.25%, XLB Materials +1.39%, XLI Industrials +1.18%, XLRE Real Estate +1.12%, XLF Financials +1.10%, XLU Utilities +0.38%, XLC Comms +0.22%, XLV Healthcare -0.13%, XLP Staples -0.66%, XLE Energy -2.43%. Top industries were JETS +6.57% and ARKG +5.05%, while bottom groups were UNG -3.45% and USO -5.68%.

THE 7s — Top baskets: Fab 7 +5.32%, Semi 7 +4.11%, Housing 7 +2.92%, Bank 7 +2.92%, Old Metals 7 +2.31%. Bottom baskets: Software 7 -0.11%, Toll Road 7 -0.30%, Old Staples 7 -0.45%, Health 7 -0.46%, Energy 7 -2.02%. Semi 7 strength was led by SOXX components (e.g., AMD strength implied by AI data) while laggards like INTU -3.95% pulled Software 7, and Energy 7 weakness clustered in gas names like FCG -2.38%.

FED & LIQUIDITY — FFR stands at 3.50%–3.75% with the next FOMC on June 17, 2026 and CME probabilities at hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0% with a committee split of 6 hawks, 5 neutrals, and 4 doves. USD liquidity stress printed 33/100 (Normal) with Reserve Scarcity at the 79th percentile the most strained component, while TED 0.09% and ON-RRP $24.9B signaled benign funding. TGA at $838.584B with a -$39.177B change added a modest positive reserve impulse.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR 1.04 and QQQ PCR 1.35 with positive skews of +9.2% and +9.1% signaled ongoing demand for downside protection despite today’s tech-led rally. IWM PCR 3.48 with skew +5.5% highlighted heavy hedging in small caps even as IWM gained +2.52%. VIX 18.1 and VXN 24.1 both sat slightly above their 20-day MAs while MOVE 85.3 (vs 72.8 MA20) flagged elevated bond-vol risk transmission.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil volatility stayed high with OVX 72.9 while USO +2.46% and Energy 7 -2.02% diverged, implying equity energy names discounted future price softness despite the day’s crude bounce. Precious metals de-risked with GLD -1.66% and SLV -4.35% even as equities rallied, reflecting the hawkish macro backdrop (CPI YoY 3.95%, PCE YoY 3.50%) pressuring duration proxies.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 50.3/100 sat Neutral. Biggest movers included JETS +6.57% (Industries), SOXX +4.74% (Industries), and AMD strength implied by AI-chip narratives, versus laggards FCG -2.38% (Industries) and INTU -3.95% (Software 7). On 20d momentum, XLK +12.05% led sectors while XLB -4.07% lagged, and baskets showed Semi 7 +33.65% best and Housing 7 -9.52% worst; vol spike names like FCG 2.8x with price down showed distribution, while MCHI 2.4x with +2.71% suggested EM accumulation on China.

WATCH — AMD (Semi 7) was implied higher within SOXX +4.74% with AI-earnings momentum and warrants watching for continuation versus elevated QQQ skew +9.1%. INTU -3.95% with 2.5x volume in Software 7 flagged potential institutional distribution or post-earnings repricing. FCG -2.38% with 2.8x volume against USO +2.46% highlighted a significant divergence between gas equities and crude that could mean either capitulation or a tactical long setup.
